meta.title: IPA Sound Drill — Master English Pronunciation with IPA
meta.description: Drill English sounds with IPA. Free browser practice for GA and RP — decode, encode, and rebuild pronunciation from the sound level.
meta.ogTitle: IPA Sound Drill — Drill English Sounds with IPA
meta.ogDescription: A free, open IPA pronunciation trainer. Decode, encode, and listen your way to clearer English — GA and RP accents supported.

lead_html: Drill IPA reading and writing for words you already know—sound by sound. Target trap sounds (θ, ð, æ, ʒ, ɝ) or your weak spots from practice history. Scored by accuracy only—build IPA coverage, not longer word lists.
lead_connected_html: Hear connected speech in GA—linking, assimilation, and elision. Read the connected IPA and recover the original phrase.
lead_weak_html: Hear the weak forms of function words in connected speech. Read the IPA and recover the word.

modeb.title: Listen & learn
modeb.lead_html: Learn new words from sound—hear the word, then match meaning and spelling. Progress by CEFR band with spaced review.

dir.label: Direction
dir.decode_d: Read the IPA and type the word
dir.encode_d: Build the IPA from the written word

wordlist_fail: Couldn't load the word list. Refresh and try again.
build_ph: Tap the IPA keys below to build the IPA

note.schwa: Schwa (ə/ɚ): keep it weak and vague—don't let spelling lead you

summary.weak_none_d: You didn't miss any sounds. Try a higher level.

top.tagline: Retune your English. From sound up.

about.title: About this app
about.placeholder: A learning tool to verify English pronunciation with IPA and rebuild it from sound.
about.lead: IPA Sound Drill is a training tool for sharpening English sounds through the phonetic alphabet (IPA). It's built for adult learners who want to raise the resolution of both their listening and their speech — with GA (American) and RP (British) accents supported.
about.why_ipa_html:
For adult learners, listening and pronunciation are inseparable. A sound you can't produce accurately is a sound you can't reliably distinguish by ear — and a sound you can't distinguish is one you can't reproduce. Both are two sides of the same ability: perceiving sound as sound.

Take the word turtle. Its actual English pronunciation is /ˈtɝːtəl/ — two syllables built on an r-colored vowel and a schwa, quite unlike how the spelling suggests it should sound. The gap between how a word looks and how it truly sounds is where pronunciation breaks down.

To make English your own, you need to handle sound at IPA-level resolution: not just "ah" versus "ee," but /æ/, /ʌ/, /ɑ/, and /ə/ as distinct sounds you can both hear apart and pronounce apart.

There are a few ways to reach that resolution: living in an English-speaking environment, doing most of your learning through English audio, or studying IPA deliberately. For adult learners where the first two aren't realistic, IPA study is by far the most time-efficient path.

IPA Sound Drill trains this "sound resolution" phoneme by phoneme. It focuses on the trap sounds learners struggle with most — θ, ð, æ, ʒ, ɝ — building the foundation for everything that comes next in your English.

features.title: What this app offers
features.item_1_html: Four learning drills. Practice is split into four drills: "Check the sound" (IPA → word), "Write from pronunciation" (word → IPA), "Learn words from sound" (SRS vocabulary), and "Get used to connected sounds" (connected speech / weak forms) — each targeting a different goal.
features.item_2_html: 5,397 CEFR-tagged words. Every word carries a CEFR tag (A1 / A2 / B1 / B2), so you can filter down to just the words that match your level.
features.item_3_html: Focused trap-sound practice. Drills target the trap sounds learners find hardest (θ / ð / æ / ʒ / ɝ), and automatically surface your weak spots from practice history — growing symbol coverage efficiently.
features.item_4_html: Search words by IPA symbol. Build a string of IPA symbols from the palette to search for words. Type æk, for example, and every word containing that sound — "back," "pack," "lack" — appears. Arranged by phonetic category (10 groups of vowels and consonants), the palette doubles as a phonetics reference.
features.item_5_html: See your progress. A progress meter shows how far you've come in each of the four drills, with a three-mark system (three graduation checks per word) tracking mastery. An SRS review queue makes it clear at a glance what needs reviewing now.

about.contact_html: For feedback and bug reports, please use the feedback form.
